behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is at the wiring boundary between JSON parsing and downstream hook-input handling (escaping/backticks/$vars carried through intact). A pure unit test of only the parser could miss breakage in handoff/normalization between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real hook input handler stack in-process (no mocks for parser or input-routing modules) with multiple valid markdown-heavy JSON payloads (code fences, inline backticks, dollar-prefixed tokens) and assert: parse success, no json_parse_failed trace, and expected downstream handler state/event.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: The key failure boundary is parser + trace-emission contract. Unit tests can miss missing/incorrect trace fields if emission is done in another module or adapter.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON inputs into the real parse-and-trace path in-process and assert a `json_parse_failed` record is emitted with required diagnostic fields (e.g., error message/type, position/offset or snippet, correlation metadata).

behaviors.description: On parse failure, the hook exits in a non-crashing way while still recording the failure signal for later analysis.
behaviors.justification: Non-crashing exit is process-level behavior (uncaught exception vs controlled exit code/path). In-process tests may miss real termination behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the hook entrypoint as a subprocess with malformed JSON on stdin; assert process does not crash (controlled exit semantics), and failure signal (`json_parse_failed`) is persisted/emitted for later analysis.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly targets real stdin plumbing and trace artifact generation via actual OS/subprocess and sink I/O boundaries; mocks could hide integration defects.
behaviors.test_description: Run a full hook invocation via subprocess, send malformed payload over stdin, and assert the actual trace sink artifact (file/stream record) contains expected `json_parse_failed` entry and context.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: The discrimination logic sits in parser/error-classification + trace signaling across modules. This does not require external systems if exercised through the real in-process stack.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired regression cases (valid markdown-heavy JSON vs structurally malformed JSON) through the same in-process hook-input pipeline and assert mutually exclusive outcomes: valid case follows normal handling with no parse-failure trace; malformed case emits `json_parse_failed` with diagnostics.
